questions: Should the scheduler post at fixed times (e.g., every day at 2pm UTC) or respect per-post scheduled_for timestamps? The plan assumes per-post timestamps for maximum flexibility, but a simpler 'daily batch at X time' approach is also viable.

For retweets/quote tweets: Twitter API v2 supports quote tweets (pass quote_tweet_id to create_tweet). Should we support native retweets (just amplifying someone else's tweet) or only quote tweets (your own text + embedded tweet)?

Should there be a confirmation step before scheduled posts go live? E.g., the bot DMs admin 30 min before posting for final approval, or should queued posts publish automatically without further interaction?

When scheduling posts sourced from Discord messages, should the bot resolve and cache the media URL at schedule time (to avoid expired CDN URLs), or download-on-publish (simpler but Discord CDN URLs expire)?
Is there a desired daily posting cadence/limit? E.g., max 3 tweets per day, spaced 2+ hours apart — or just fire everything that's due?

success_criteria.criterion: A scheduled_posts table exists in Supabase with the required columns and an index on (status, scheduled_for)
Admin can schedule a tweet via DM with the bot (schedule_tweet tool) specifying tweet text, optional media source, optional reply target, and a publish time
A tasks.loop scheduler in SharingCog polls for due posts and publishes them using the existing sharing pipeline (finalize_sharing or post_tweet)
Posted tweets are recorded in shared_posts and announced to the announcement channel, same as manually triggered tweets
Admin can list pending scheduled posts and cancel them via DM tools
Failed posts are marked as failed with error details and admin is notified via DM
Thread replies work: admin can schedule a reply to an existing tweet ID
Text-only tweets work: admin can schedule a tweet with just text and no media source
Scheduler loop runs every 1 minute and correctly identifies posts where scheduled_for <= now()
No duplicate posting: status transitions (pending -> posting -> posted/failed) prevent double-publishing
scheduled_posts is in QUERYABLE_TABLES so admin can inspect it via query_table
schedule_tweet supports relative time inputs like 'tomorrow 2pm' in addition to ISO timestamps
Each new file stays under ~300 lines; existing files grow minimally
No changes to existing social_poster.post_tweet or Sharer.finalize_sharing signatures
Manual end-to-end test: schedule a post 2 min in the future, verify it publishes correctly

assumptions: The admin is the sole user of the scheduling system — no multi-user queue management needed.
Per-post scheduled_for timestamps (not a fixed daily batch time) — this is more flexible and the 1-min polling loop is cheap.
Posts publish automatically when due, without a pre-publish confirmation step from admin.

Media is downloaded at publish time (not schedule time) to keep the queue lightweight — Discord CDN URLs from recent messages should still be valid within a day or two. If this is a problem, we can add prefetch later.
Native retweets are out of scope; only original tweets, replies, and quote tweets are supported.
No rate limiting beyond what Twitter enforces — admin is responsible for not over-scheduling.
The Supabase migration will be applied manually (or via existing migration tooling) — there's no automated migration runner in the bot.
quote_tweet_url support requires passing quote_tweet_id to tweepy's create_tweet — this is a small addition to social_poster.post_tweet but is worth including.
The scheduler runs in the same process as the Discord bot via discord.ext.tasks — no separate worker process needed.
structure_warnings: Each step section should reference at least one file in backticks.
